assertions.description: Document.prototype.requestStorageAccess is the API whose semantics now follow strict same-origin instead of same-site.
Document.prototype.hasStorageAccess returns a Promise<boolean>; the strict same-origin change affects what counts as 'has'.
Document.prototype.requestStorageAccessFor is the top-level surface used by sites to request access on behalf of an origin (the spec narrows what is granted).
CookieStore is exposed; cookie attachment to subresource requests is the underlying behaviour the Storage Access API gates.
location.origin returns a string origin (scheme + host + port), the unit of comparison under strict same-origin policy.
navigator.permissions.query accepts the storage-access descriptor name (used to check policy after the strict change).
